commands.review_summary:
The branch separates Today from Latest bucket selection for local cost snapshots and OpenAI/Claude Admin API usage surfaces, while adding regression coverage for stale histories and UTC bucket rollover cases.

Reproducibility: yes. Current main is source-reproducible because token snapshots select the newest parsed daily entry and Admin API menu summaries use latestDay under Today-labeled UI; I did not run tests because this review is read-only.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] Human follow-up is needed because the remaining blocker is contributor-side real behavior proof or maintainer acceptance of the test-only proof, not a repairable code defect.

target.title: [codex] Fix stale today usage buckets
target.body:
## Summary
- Use the current local calendar day for usage surfaces labeled Today instead of the newest historical bucket.
- For OpenAI and Claude Admin API UTC daily buckets, select the bucket whose `[startTime, endTime)` contains the refresh timestamp, so positive-timezone morning usage is included without summing adjacent UTC buckets after rollover.
- Share bucket/day lookup helpers across local cost snapshots plus OpenAI and Claude Admin API snapshots.
- Preserve explicit latest-bucket behavior for Bedrock and Mistral, and add regression coverage for stale histories, positive-timezone morning usage, and adjacent UTC bucket overcounting.

## Root Cause
Daily usage history can be sparse or stale, but several UI paths labeled the newest available daily bucket as Today. That allowed yesterday or older usage to appear as current-day usage. For Admin API buckets, selecting by bucket label/start day also missed the UTC bucket that contains the local morning for users east of UTC. A broader overlap-based fix included that morning bucket, but could double-count adjacent UTC buckets later in the day.

## Impact
When no current-day usage bucket exists, Today now renders zero cost/tokens instead of stale values. For OpenAI and Claude Admin API history, Today now uses the single UTC bucket active at refresh time, which matches the provider's UTC aggregation boundary while avoiding double-counted spend/tokens. The fix is lightweight: it selects from already-parsed history and does not add network calls or broad scanning.

## Review Proof
Focused deterministic proof for the timezone edge cases:

```text
swift test --filter AdminAPIUsageLocalDaySelectionTests

Suite AdminAPIUsageLocalDaySelectionTests started.
Test "OpenAI current day includes UTC bucket containing positive timezone morning" passed.
Test "OpenAI current day does not sum adjacent UTC buckets after positive timezone UTC rollover" passed.
Test "Claude Admin current day includes UTC bucket containing positive timezone morning" passed.
Test "Claude Admin current day does not sum adjacent UTC buckets after negative timezone UTC rollover" passed.
Suite AdminAPIUsageLocalDaySelectionTests passed.
Test run with 4 tests in 1 suite passed.
```

This is data-free equivalent runtime proof for the reviewed behavior; I did not run live provider probes because they may require real account credentials or keychain prompts.

## Validation
- `make test` passed all 43 shards.
- `swift test --filter AdminAPIUsageLocalDaySelectionTests` passed.
- `swift test --filter OpenAIAPIUsageFetcherTests` passed.
- `swift test --filter ClaudeAdminAPIUsageTests` passed.
- `swift test --filter OpenAIAPIMenuCardModelTests` passed.
- `swift test --filter ClaudeAdminAPIInlineDashboardModelTests` passed.
- `swiftformat Sources Tests --lint` passed.
- `swiftlint --strict` passed.
- `git diff --check` passed.

Fixes #1705
